wine_bars_late.name: Wine Bar at Les Halles d'Avignon
wine_bars_late.wine_program: By-the-glass selection of 30 to 50 Rhone Valley wines at the indoor covered market; southern Rhone focus with all major appellations represented
wine_bars_late.description: The wine bar inside Avignon's covered Les Halles market operates into the evening on market nights. A curated selection of Chateauneuf-du-Pape, Gigondas, Tavel rose, and Cotes du Rhone by the glass alongside regional cheese and charcuterie boards.
wine_bars_late.tip: The market is liveliest on Saturday and Sunday evenings. Chef-led tapas stands around the perimeter serve ideal wine-pairing bites.

wine_bars_late.name: L'Epice and Wine
wine_bars_late.address: 14 rue des Lices, 84000 Avignon, Vaucluse, France
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Sommelier-curated list of 200 bottles and 20 by-the-glass pours focusing on Rhone Valley natural and biodynamic producers
wine_bars_late.description: One of Avignon's most wine-focused bars, L'Epice and Wine opens late with a slate of natural Rhone wines by the glass, organic snacks, and a knowledgeable team. The bottle list extends to producers including Gramenon and Texier alongside the more familiar Beaucastel and Vieux Telegraphe.
wine_bars_late.tip: Ask the sommelier for a Grenache Noir vertical from Chateauneuf to Gigondas to Cotes du Rhone Villages to track the appellation quality ladder in a single session.

wine_bars_late.name: Le Carillon wine bar
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Southern Rhone by-the-glass programme from a terrace on Avignon's most atmospheric piazza; 15 to 20 pours nightly
wine_bars_late.description: Place Crillon is Avignon's main evening gathering point, and Le Carillon is one of the bars on this grand square with a genuine wine programme rather than just cocktails. By-the-glass Chateauneuf, Gigondas, and Tavel rose on the terrace with views of the illuminated city walls.
wine_bars_late.tip: Arrive before 21:00 for a terrace table. The square fills with evening promenaders after the Palais des Papes light show in summer.

wine_bars_late.name: Winebar Les Bacchantes
wine_bars_late.wine_program: 120 bottles on the slate, 40 by-the-glass, focus on northern Rhone (Cote-Rotie, Condrieu, Hermitage) and natural Rhone producers
wine_bars_late.description: Les Bacchantes is one of Lyon's most serious wine bars, with a northern Rhone and Beaujolais focus that complements the city's position as the gateway to the Rhone corridor. Late-night by-the-glass pours including entry-level Guigal Cote-Rotie and Condrieu cuvees alongside Crozes-Hermitage and Saint-Joseph.
wine_bars_late.tip: Pair the Syrah tasting flight with a plate of Lyonnaise rosette or cervelas sausage for a complete northern Rhone food-and-wine context.

wine_bars_late.name: La Cave des Bulles
wine_bars_late.wine_program: Northern Rhone specialist wine bar with a focus on Crozes-Hermitage, Hermitage, and Saint-Peray sparkling; 25 by-the-glass
wine_bars_late.description: Valence, the capital of the Drome department and the gateway to the Hermitage zone, hosts this specialist wine bar focusing on Crozes-Hermitage, Hermitage, and Saint-Peray. The house speciality is the Saint-Peray sparkling Marsanne by the glass, one of the few places outside the estate cellars to find it.
wine_bars_late.tip: Ask for the tasting flight of three Saint-Peray styles (still, sparkling cremant, and sparkling reserve) to understand why the appellation is underrated.

listening_bars.name: Le Mistral
listening_bars.address: 11 rue du Portail Matheron, 84000 Avignon, Vaucluse, France
listening_bars.wine_program: Natural wine list of 80 bottles and 12 by-the-glass; vinyl soundtrack; southern Rhone focus
listening_bars.description: Le Mistral is a vinyl-and-wine concept bar inside the Avignon walls: a curated natural wine list with southern Rhone wines as the backbone, played alongside a vinyl record collection covering jazz, afrobeat, and French chanson. Small plates of regional charcuterie.
listening_bars.tip: Arrive after 22:00 for the late-night vinyl session. The natural wine list rotates monthly and features small producers not stocked elsewhere in Avignon.

listening_bars.name: L'Oenomane
listening_bars.address: 8 rue de la Charité, 69002 Lyon, Rhone, France
listening_bars.wine_program: Biodynamic and natural wine specialist; 200 bottles and 18 by-the-glass; northern Rhone and Beaujolais heavy; live jazz on Thursdays
listening_bars.description: One of Lyon's best natural wine bars, L'Oenomane on the Presqu'ile hosts live jazz evenings alongside a deep northern Rhone wine list that includes domaines not available anywhere else in Lyon: Gonon, Texier, Romaneaux-Destezet. The format is convivial and the staff speak English.
listening_bars.tip: Thursday jazz evenings are popular; arrive by 20:00 to secure a seat. The white Condrieu and Saint-Joseph Marsanne selection is particularly strong.

candle_lit.name: La Mirande Hotel Bar
candle_lit.address: 4 place de l'Amirande, 84000 Avignon, Vaucluse, France
candle_lit.wine_program: Hotel bar wine list of 300 bottles; Chateauneuf-du-Pape verticals; rare old-vintage Rhone reds and whites by the glass on rotation
candle_lit.description: La Mirande is Avignon's most romantic hotel, a 14th-century cardinal's palace adjacent to the Palais des Papes, restored to period grandeur. Its candlelit bar pours rare Chateauneuf-du-Pape verticals and old-vintage Hermitage by the glass in one of the most atmospheric settings in southern France. Non-residents are welcome at the bar.
candle_lit.tip: Ask the sommelier for the cellar's Chateauneuf old-vintage selection. Beaucastel, Rayas, and Vieux Telegraphe verticals are occasionally available by the glass at premium but reasonable prices.

candle_lit.name: Maison Christian Etienne restaurant bar
candle_lit.address: 10 rue de Mons, 84000 Avignon, Vaucluse, France
candle_lit.wine_program: Restaurant wine list of 400 bottles; extensive Chateauneuf-du-Pape section; bar open for wine by the glass before and after dinner service
candle_lit.description: Maison Christian Etienne, set in a 14th-century palace adjacent to the Palais des Papes, is Avignon's most celebrated restaurant and its candlelit bar offers some of the finest Chateauneuf-du-Pape by the glass in the city. Bar access is available pre-dinner and post-service for non-diners.
candle_lit.tip: The by-the-glass Chateauneuf programme rotates seasonally; in autumn the sommelier opens aged bottles from the vertical cellar.

candle_lit.name: Chateau de Rochegude hotel bar
candle_lit.wine_program: Chateau hotel wine list of 250 bottles; Cotes du Rhone and Chateauneuf emphasis; stone-vaulted medieval bar
candle_lit.description: The 12th-century Chateau de Rochegude, a Relais et Chateaux property perched above its vineyards north of Orange, has a candlelit stone-vaulted wine bar that pours its own estate wines alongside a curated Rhone selection. The setting is uniquely intimate.
candle_lit.tip: The chateau produces its own Cotes du Rhone blanc and rouge, which are poured exclusively at the property. It makes an excellent rural alternative to Avignon as a base for exploring Chateauneuf and Orange.

lounges.name: Hotel de l'Europe Grand Lounge
lounges.wine_program: Classic cellar of 200 Rhone wines; curated by-the-glass selection of 15 Chateauneuf and Cotes du Rhone Villages in the lounged inner courtyard
lounges.description: Hotel de l'Europe is Avignon's oldest hotel (1799) and its lounge bar on Place Crillon pours a serious Rhone wine list by the glass in an elegantly formal setting. Napoleon, Victor Hugo, and John Stuart Mill stayed here; the atmosphere is deliberately unhurried.
lounges.tip: The hotel courtyard is the quietest and most atmospheric wine-drinking space on Place Crillon. Ask for the seasonal Rhone vintage list rather than the standard programme.

lounges.name: L'Oustau de Baumaniere wine lounge
lounges.wine_program: One of Provence's deepest cellars: 50,000 bottles; southern Rhone vertical collection; lounge bar open to hotel guests and day visitors
lounges.description: L'Oustau de Baumaniere at Les Baux-de-Provence, 45 minutes from Avignon, holds one of the finest southern Rhone wine collections in a private setting, including rare vertical libraries of Chateauneuf-du-Pape and Gigondas. The lounge bar pours from this cellar by the glass in a palatial Provencal setting.
lounges.tip: Day visitors can access the lounge bar without a hotel stay or dinner reservation. The sommelier team will build a by-the-glass Rhone flights on request.

fortified_specialists.name: Caveau de Rasteau (Cave de Rasteau co-operative)
fortified_specialists.address: Route des Princes d'Orange, 84110 Rasteau, Vaucluse, France
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Rasteau VDN (rouge, blanc, rancio), Rasteau AOC rouge; evening tastings by arrangement through the co-operative
fortified_specialists.description: The Rasteau cave cooperative is the principal cellar for Rasteau VDN, a Grenache-based fortified wine made in rouge, blanc, and oxidative rancio styles. The rancio style, aged in glass demijohns in the sun, is one of France's most distinctive fortified wines and is rarely found outside the region. Evening opening for fortified-specialist tastings by arrangement.
fortified_specialists.tip: Request a tasting focused on the three VDN styles, culminating in the rancio. The rancio vertical (multiple vintages of oxidative aged VDN) is a unique experience that puts the Rhone's fortified tradition in context alongside Rivesaltes and Banyuls.

fortified_specialists.name: Cave de Beaumes-de-Venise: Muscat VDN evening
fortified_specialists.address: Route de Carpentras, 84190 Beaumes-de-Venise, Vaucluse, France
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise VDN by the glass; also Beaumes-de-Venise rouge AOC; cellar-door evening tastings
fortified_specialists.description: The Cave de Beaumes-de-Venise co-operative is the primary source for Muscat de Beaumes-de-Venise VDN, one of France's oldest and most aromatic fortified wines. The co-operative offers evening tastings (until 19:30) where the Muscat is poured alongside the dry Beaumes-de-Venise rouge. The aromatic intensity of fresh Muscat VDN is one of the great fortified experiences of the south.
fortified_specialists.tip: The Muscat VDN is best paired with light blue cheese (Roquefort or Fourme d'Ambert) or foie gras. Buy a case direct from the co-operative for one of the best-value VDN wines in France.

fortified_specialists.name: Domaine de la Soumade Rasteau VDN tasting room
fortified_specialists.wine_program: Rasteau VDN rouge and rancio specialist; vertical tastings by appointment; also Rasteau AOC rouge and Cotes du Rhone Villages
fortified_specialists.description: Domaine de la Soumade is one of the most respected independent producers of Rasteau VDN, offering cellar-door tastings of their fortified range including the Orondasso (barrel-aged VDN rouge) and vintage rancio. Andre Romero's cellar has been producing both dry and fortified Rasteau since the 1970s.
fortified_specialists.tip: The estate's Cotes du Rhone Villages Rasteau dry red is also worth tasting alongside the VDN. The contrast between the dry and fortified styles from the same Grenache grape shows the breadth of the appellation.

late_tastings.name: M. Chapoutier cellar and late tasting Tain-l'Hermitage
late_tastings.address: 18 avenue du Docteur Paul Durand, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage, Drome, France
late_tastings.wine_program: Biodynamic Hermitage, Crozes-Hermitage, Saint-Joseph, Cote-Rotie, Condrieu; cellar door open until 19:30 and estate events until later
late_tastings.description: M. Chapoutier's cellar shop and tasting room in Tain-l'Hermitage is one of the most accessible cellar-door tasting experiences in the northern Rhone, remaining open until 19:30. The full range from entry-level Crozes-Hermitage to the single-parcel Hermitage cuvees is available by the glass or in tasting flights. The cellar shop also stocks older vintages for purchase.
late_tastings.tip: The evening tasting just before closing (17:00-19:30) is often less crowded than mid-afternoon. Ask for the Ermitage L'Ermite white (pure Marsanne) if in stock by the glass.

verified.address_quoted: 18 avenue du Docteur Paul Durand, 26600 Tain-l'Hermitage

late_tastings.name: Inter Rhone Maison des Vins, Avignon
late_tastings.address: Maison du Tourisme, 41 cours Jean Jaures, 84000 Avignon, Vaucluse, France
late_tastings.wine_program: Official Inter Rhone tasting space presenting the full range of Rhone Valley appellations; up to 80 wines available by the glass or tasting flight
late_tastings.description: Inter Rhone operates a representative tasting space in Avignon presenting the full span of Rhone Valley appellations, from Cote-Rotie to Tavel, with structured tasting flights designed for first-time visitors and specialists alike. An ideal orientation session before exploring the region's cellar doors.
late_tastings.tip: The Rhone Valley appellation comparison flight (covering 6 appellations across north and south) is the most instructive single tasting of the corridor and costs under EUR25.

late_tastings.name: E. Guigal cellar door evening, Ampuis
late_tastings.address: 1 route de Larnage, 69420 Ampuis, Rhone, France
late_tastings.wine_program: Cote-Rotie, Condrieu, Hermitage, Chateauneuf-du-Pape; the full Guigal range from Cotes du Rhone up to La La La prestige cuvees; evening by appointment
late_tastings.description: Guigal's cellar door in Ampuis offers the complete Guigal portfolio tasting, from the Cotes du Rhone entry wines up through the Cote-Rotie La Mouline, La Landonne, and La Turque prestige cuvees. Evening appointments are available until 19:00 for groups who book in advance.
late_tastings.tip: The prestige cuvees (La La La) are available by the glass only in private tasting sessions booked separately. Request the dedicated La La La experience when booking.

late_tastings.name: Domaine du Vieux Telegraphe walk-in tasting, Bedarrides
late_tastings.address: 3 route de Chateauneuf-du-Pape, 84370 Bedarrides, Vaucluse, France
late_tastings.wine_program: Chateauneuf-du-Pape La Crau and standard blend; also Gigondas and Cotes du Rhone from Brunier family
late_tastings.description: Domaine du Vieux Telegraphe at Bedarrides accepts walk-in tastings at the cellar door without appointment, making it the most accessible of the major Chateauneuf-du-Pape estates. The late-afternoon session before the 18:30 closing is typically quieter and allows for a more extended conversation with the cellar staff.
late_tastings.tip: The La Crau (single-parcel old-vine Grenache blend from the La Crau plateau) is the reference cuvee. Compare it with the standard blend to understand what single-parcel selection adds.

sparkling_rooms.name: Domaine du Tunnel Saint-Peray sparkling cellar, Saint-Peray
sparkling_rooms.address: 20 rue de la Republique, 07130 Saint-Peray, Ardeche, France
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Saint-Peray AOC sparkling Marsanne methode traditionnelle; also still Saint-Peray and Cornas; cellar-door tasting by appointment
sparkling_rooms.description: Stephane Robert's Domaine du Tunnel produces the finest Saint-Peray sparkling Marsanne in the appellation, a methode traditionnelle white using Marsanne on granite soils. Saint-Peray sparkling is a 19th-century tradition revived by a handful of committed producers; tasting it at the cellar door in Saint-Peray is one of the most distinctive wine experiences in the northern Rhone.
sparkling_rooms.tip: Saint-Peray sparkling Marsanne is nothing like Champagne: it is broader, richer, and more textured, with honey and beeswax alongside stone fruit. A uniquely Rhone experience.

sparkling_rooms.name: Cave Jaillance Clairette de Die sparkling room
sparkling_rooms.address: Avenue de la Clairette, 26150 Die, Drome, France
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Clairette de Die AOC Methode Ancestrale sparkling (Muscat and Clairette); also Cremant de Die and still Coteaux de Die whites
sparkling_rooms.description: Die in the Drome department produces Clairette de Die, a low-alcohol (7-8 percent ABV) naturally sweet sparkling wine made by the Methode Ancestrale (partially fermented in bottle). Cave Jaillance, the principal co-operative, has a dedicated sparkling wine tasting room in Die open for visits. The Clairette de Die Methode Ancestrale is one of France's most unusual and historically resonant sparkling wines.
sparkling_rooms.tip: Clairette de Die is nothing like Prosecco despite its sweetness level. The Muscat-dominant Methode Ancestrale has a unique grape-skin freshness that chills beautifully for summer aperitifs. Die is also a spectacular mountain town worth the detour.

sparkling_rooms.name: Delas Freres wine salon, Saint-Jean-de-Muzols
sparkling_rooms.address: ZA de l'Olivet, 07300 Saint-Jean-de-Muzols, Ardeche, France
sparkling_rooms.neighborhood: Saint-Jean-de-Muzols, Ardeche (across the river from Tain)
sparkling_rooms.wine_program: Full Delas portfolio; Hermitage, Crozes-Hermitage, Saint-Joseph; the cellar door also pours the sparkling Saint-Joseph Methode Traditionnelle in season
sparkling_rooms.description: Delas Freres, one of the northern Rhone's oldest houses (founded 1835), produces a Saint-Joseph Methode Traditionnelle sparkling white in addition to its full Syrah and white range. The cellar door in Saint-Jean-de-Muzols offers access to the full portfolio including this unusual northern Rhone sparkling.
sparkling_rooms.tip: The Delas Saint-Joseph sparkling (Marsanne-Roussanne, methode traditionnelle) is produced in small quantities and rarely found outside the estate. It provides a fascinating comparison with the still Hermitage whites.
